summary: Iteration 2 backend testing complete. All 29 new tests for CMS, Item Master (+bulk CSV upload), Stores (bulk/edit/delete/sample), Analytics dashboards (sales/customer/loyalty/campaign/store/nps), transaction drill-down, coupon-detail, ticket get-single + notes, coupon PATCH, and customer churn_risk filter — PASS. Iteration 1 regression (54 tests) also re-run and all pass. Total: 83/83 passing.

minor.issue: Performs $set with the raw payload at top-level, which means a partial PUT (e.g. only {'home': {...}}) will REPLACE the entire 'home' subdocument rather than deep-merging. Acceptable for a full-form save but caller must always send the complete section. Document this behaviour.

Accept untyped dict payloads with no Pydantic validation — relies on duck-typing. float(row.get('mrp')) etc. will raise on bad input and is caught generically. Consider Pydantic models for stronger contracts/error reporting.
Hard-delete (deletes the document). Inconsistent with /api/stores/{id} which is soft-delete (is_active=false). Choose one strategy across resource types.
Inserts items in a sequential loop with per-row find_one+insert_one. For large CSVs this is slow. Consider bulk_write or insert_many after dedupe.
Uses $dateFromString on every transaction — if any bill_date string is malformed the aggregation will fail. Consider storing bill_date as native Date or guarding with $convert.
Inconsistent URL patterns for the same concept (one has /download suffix, the other doesn't). Standardise.

action_items: (Optional) Standardise CSV-sample endpoint paths between items and stores.
(Optional) Decide between soft-delete vs hard-delete consistently across items, stores, customers.
(Optional) Use Pydantic models for items/categories/cms PUT payloads instead of raw dict for validation + OpenAPI docs.
(Optional) Document that PUT /api/cms/content replaces top-level sections wholesale (no deep merge).
critical_code_review_comments: All routes correctly project out _id ({'_id':0}) — no ObjectId leakage.
Auth gating uses require_roles(*MANAGEMENT_ROLES/ADMIN_ROLES) consistently on mutating endpoints; CMS GET correctly left public.
Bulk upload endpoints correctly de-dupe (by sku for items, by code for stores) and return {inserted, skipped, errors}.
Transaction-detail returns embedded {transaction, customer, store} — denormalised reads ok for drill-down but expect N+1 if invoked in a loop.
Analytics aggregations rely on $substr / $dateFromString on string dates — works but fragile; switch to native Date type would be cleaner and faster.
ticket notes endpoint always returns 200 regardless of whether the ticket exists — should 404 if no document matched (currently silently no-ops).
delete_category and delete_item perform no existence check — return 200 even for unknown id. Consider returning 404 when matched_count == 0.
seed_data_creation: Test data prefixed with TEST_ created during run: ~2 items via CRUD, ~2 items via bulk upload, 1 store via bulk upload (then soft-deleted), 1 category (deleted), 1 ticket with 1 note, CMS hero_eyebrow temporarily mutated to a TEST_ marker (left in DB — main agent should reset CMS via UI/PUT if needed). Coupon name restored after PATCH test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/iteration2_test.py /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v` for full regression (83 tests). Both files are independent and idempotent. iteration2_test.py covers all new iteration-2 endpoints (CMS, items, stores bulk/edit, analytics dashboards, ticket get+notes, coupon PATCH, customer filter). The CMS content was overwritten with a TEST_ hero_eyebrow marker — if running tests changes user-visible content on the public site, consider resetting via UI.
rca of the issue: N/A — no production bugs found. All iteration-2 endpoints behave per spec.
